summary: Dan Shapiro shares that six years after missing a chance to donate bone marrow due to inbox neglect, he has been matched again as a potential donor. He reflects on the deep regret of his earlier failure to respond and expresses gratitude for a second opportunity. The post serves as both a personal update and a call to action for readers to sign up as marrow donors.
key_points: Shapiro was matched as a marrow donor years ago but missed it because he didn't check his mail, calling it one of his deepest regrets
He has now been matched a second time and is going in for testing to confirm he can help
He urges readers to sign up or update their contact info at marrow.org
money_quotes: I missed my chance to save the life of another human being because I didn't check the mail.
I've never forgotten that, and it's one of my deepest regrets.
key_insight: Small acts of negligence like ignoring your inbox can have life-or-death consequences, but sometimes you get a rare second chance to make it right.
